name: El Saltillo Trail
name_original: Sendero El Saltillo – trail run desde Canillas del Aceituno
description: Jog the El Saltillo Trail, a moderate 4.4-mile route with 787 feet of gain, featuring stunning gorge views and a suspension bridge.
region_intro: Jogging routes around Canillas De Aceituno are situated in the foothills of the Sierra Tejeda, offering a mountainous backdrop. The region is characterized by diverse landscapes, including olive groves, vineyards, and natural springs feeding rivers like the Almanchares. Elevated positions provide panoramic views of the La Viñuela reservoir and the Mediterranean coast. Best jogging routes around Canillas De Aceituno The most popular jogging route is El Saltillo Trail, a 4.4 miles (7.0 km) trail that takes 58 minutes to complete. This route features stunning gorge views and connects to the village of Sedella. Another top favourite among local runners is Maroma – Cueva de la Rábita loop from Canillas de Aceituno, a difficult 11.5 miles (18.5 km) path. This route ascends through mountainous terrain, offering significant elevation gain. Local runners also love the El Atalayón – Cueva de la Rábita loop from Canillas de Aceituno, a 14.5 miles (23.2 km) trail leading through varied mountain landscapes, often completed in about 3 hours 50 minutes. Jogging around Canillas De Aceituno is defined by mountainous terrain, olive groves, and panoramic viewpoints. The network offers options for both moderate and challenging ability levels. The routes in Canillas De Aceituno are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 3.9 stars from over 14 reviews. More than 300 runners have used komoot to explore Canillas De Aceituno's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available in Canillas De Aceituno?
A: Komoot features around 14 dedicated running routes in Canillas De Aceituno. These routes cater to various fitness levels, though most are considered challenging due to the mountainous terrain.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on the jogging trails around Canillas De Aceituno?
A: The running trails in Canillas De Aceituno are predominantly mountainous, offering a mix of challenging ascents and descents. You'll jog through diverse landscapes including olive groves, vineyards, and areas fed by natural springs, providing a varied and scenic experience.

Q: Are there any easy running routes for beginners in Canillas De Aceituno?
A: While most routes in Canillas De Aceituno are rated as difficult, there is at least one moderate option available. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e996268636/el-saltillo-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Saltillo Trail&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 7 km path with stunning gorge views, making it a good choice for those looking for a less strenuous run.

Q: What are the most scenic viewpoints or landmarks I can see while running in the area?
A: Canillas De Aceituno offers breathtaking panoramic views. Many routes provide expansive vistas of the La Viñuela reservoir and even the Mediterranean coast. A major highlight is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3895596" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Saltillo Suspension Bridge&lt;/a&gt;, the third longest hanging bridge in Spain, which is part of several popular running paths. You can also encounter various peaks like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/448453" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Maroma Summit&lt;/a&gt; and gorges like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3889477" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Walkway Through the Río Algarrobo Gorge&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any circular running routes in Canillas De Aceituno?
A: Yes, several popular running routes in Canillas De Aceituno are circular. Examples include the challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24520207" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Maroma – Cueva de la Rábita loop&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/19351087" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Saltillo Suspension Bridge – Canillas de Aceituno loop&lt;/a&gt;, which offers a great way to experience the region's natural beauty.

Q: What do other runners say about the trails in Canillas De Aceituno?
A: The running routes in Canillas De Aceituno are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 3.9 stars from over 14 reviews. Runners often praise the challenging mountainous terrain, the diverse landscapes including olive groves and vineyards, and the stunning panoramic views of the reservoir and coast.

Q: Are there any routes that pass by natural caves?
A: Yes, some running routes in the area incorporate natural caves. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20118701" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cueva del Agua – Cueva de la Rábita loop&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 10.8 km route that takes you past these interesting geological features, adding another dimension to your run.

Q: What is the longest running route available in Canillas De Aceituno?
A: One of the longest and most challenging running routes is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38144402" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Atalayón – Cueva de la Rábita loop from Canillas de Aceituno&lt;/a&gt;. This difficult trail spans approximately 23.2 km and features significant elevation gain, making it suitable for experienced runners seeking a long-distance challenge.

Q: Is Canillas De Aceituno a good place for trail running with significant elevation gain?
A: Absolutely. Canillas De Aceituno is nestled in the foothills of the Sierra Tejeda, with Mount Maroma, the highest peak in Málaga province, within its boundaries. This provides an excellent environment for trail running with substantial elevation changes. Many routes, like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24520207" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Maroma – Cueva de la Rábita loop&lt;/a&gt;, feature over 1400 meters of ascent, offering a demanding workout.

Q: Are there any running events or races held in Canillas De Aceituno?
A: Yes, Canillas De Aceituno hosts an annual urban race called the 'Carrera Urbana Canillas de Aceituno «El Saltillo»'. This event features a 6,200-meter course that winds through the village's main streets and emblematic points, showcasing the community's engagement with running.

Q: What makes the El Saltillo area special for runners?
A: The El Saltillo area is a major draw for runners due to its unique features. It's home to the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3895596" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Saltillo Suspension Bridge&lt;/a&gt;, which is a significant landmark. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e996268636/el-saltillo-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Saltillo Trail&lt;/a&gt; itself offers stunning gorge views and connects Canillas de Aceituno to the village of Sedella, providing a memorable running experience.
tips: For the break on site, you should have a jacket with you when you are not on the road in midsummer, because it is pretty cool there.

The trail runs through the Río de los Almanchares canyon with amazing views along the entire route from the town of Canillas del Aceituno to El Saltillo. Be careful, there are sectors with loose stone.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the El Saltillo Trail?
faqs.answer: The trail typically starts from the village of Canillas de Aceituno. You can find parking within the village, though it might be limited, especially during peak times. Arriving early is recommended to secure a spot.

faqs.question: Is the El Saltillo Trail suitable for jogging, given its difficulty?
faqs.answer: While the komoot tour is categorized for jogging, the El Saltillo Trail is generally considered challenging due to its rugged mountain terrain, narrow paths, and steep sections. It's more suited for experienced and very fit trail runners, or those prepared to hike significant portions. It's not recommended for beginner joggers.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on the El Saltillo Trail?
faqs.answer: The terrain is rugged and varied. You'll encounter narrow paths, some with loose stone, and sections alongside traditional irrigation channels (acequias). There are steep slopes, including a descent to the suspension bridge and an ascent just after, where chains are provided for assistance. Metal walkways built on rocky overhangs are also part of the route. Good hiking shoes are essential for sure-footedness.

faqs.question: What are the main highlights and viewpoints along the route?
faqs.answer: The trail offers spectacular panoramic views of olive groves, the village of Canillas de Aceituno, and the deep gorge of the Almanchares river. A major highlight is the impressive El Saltillo Trail (views) itself, featuring its iconic suspension bridge. You'll also pass the beautiful El Saltillo Waterfall. On clear days, you might even spot the Mediterranean Sea and the continent of Africa from higher points.

faqs.question: Is the El Saltillo Trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: The trail's challenging terrain, including narrow paths, steep sections with chains, and high suspension bridges, makes it generally unsuitable for dogs. It's best to leave your furry friends at home for this adventure.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to visit the El Saltillo Trail?
faqs.answer: Spring and autumn are generally the best times to visit. The weather is milder, making the challenging sections more manageable. Summers can be very hot, and some sections might be exposed to direct sunlight. Winter can bring colder temperatures and potentially slippery conditions on certain parts of the trail.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or is there an entrance fee for the El Saltillo Trail?
faqs.answer: Currently, there is no entrance fee or permit required to access the El Saltillo Trail. It is part of the Sierra de Tejeda, Almijara and Alhama Natural Park, which is free to enter. However, always check local regulations before your visit, as conditions can change.

faqs.question: Does the El Saltillo Trail connect with other routes?
faqs.answer: Yes, the El Saltillo Trail is part of the Gran Senda de Málaga (GR-249). The route also passes through sections of the official trails such as Sendero El Saltillo, Puente Colgante El Saltillo, and Camino al Saltillo.

faqs.question: Are there any specific sections of the trail that are particularly challenging or require extra caution?
faqs.answer: Yes, the trail includes several technically challenging sections. There's a steep descent to the main suspension bridge and an equally steep ascent immediately after, where chains are provided to assist you. The trail also features narrow stretches along steep drops and metal walkways built on rocky overhangs. It is not recommended for people with vertigo.

faqs.question: Is the El Saltillo Trail a loop or an out-and-back route?
faqs.answer: The most common and recommended way to experience the El Saltillo Trail is as an out-and-back route from Canillas de Aceituno. You can turn back after crossing the main suspension bridge for a slightly shorter experience, or continue further before returning the same way.

faqs.question: What should I bring for a trip on the El Saltillo Trail?
faqs.answer: Given the rugged terrain and challenging nature, it's crucial to bring sturdy hiking shoes with good grip, plenty of water, snacks, sun protection (hat, sunscreen), and a first-aid kit. Trekking poles can also be helpful for stability on steep or loose sections. Remember to carry out all your rubbish.
